guards: Rendering closeout_trust must not submit a fabricated final response.
Reject terminal finals while the terminal outcome contract state is CONTINUE.
--executor-command is the package-owned ordinary host boundary: it must admit executor stdout before exposing final text.
Rejected CONTINUE attempts must keep custody with the agent and re-enter the executor after the selected continuation operation.
Persist only ignored local checkpoint state under .agentic-workspace/local/.
Do not treat local admission slices as closure evidence.

steps.description: Execute the operation through the generated TypeScript domain operation table.

ir_plan.summary: Generated TypeScript native runtime binding for a command whose source operation has not yet been decomposed into portable IR.

proof: final-response admission tests execute the generated CLI command across multiple ordinary executor slices
codex-sbx host adapter submits the output-last-message artifact through final-response admit before the harness can read it
local checkpoint schema accepts persisted admission slices without making them completion evidence
closeout rendering exposes the integrated vendor-neutral ordinary executor loop and the codex-sbx dogfooding adapter

steps.description: Evaluate the terminal outcome contract against the model-authored final response attempt; when --executor-command is supplied, repeat executor stdout admission after rejected CONTINUE attempts.
Run the selected ordinary continuation operation when the final response is not authorized.
Persist the local admission and resume slice.

summary: Admit or reject a model-authored final response, or run a vendor-neutral executor loop that re-enters execution while CONTINUE remains.

writes.when: persist the host admission and continuation slice
